The [steam locomotive](/wiki/Steam_locomotive "Steam locomotive") class **k.u.k HB 578** was a [tank engine](/wiki/Tank_engine "Tank engine") operated by the Imperial and Royal Military Field Railway (*k.u.k. Heeresfeldbahn*) in [Austria-Hungary](/wiki/Austria-Hungary "Austria-Hungary").

In [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I"), the Austrian Military Railway placed their order for additional locomotives with [Henschel](/wiki/Henschel-Werke "Henschel-Werke") in [Kassel](/wiki/Kassel "Kassel"), because the Austrian locomotive factories were working at full capacity. These 22 locomotives had typical Prussian features and were grouped into **Class 578**. After the war's end, 10 engines were left in [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland") as **PKP Class TKp101** and twelve with the [BBÖ](/wiki/BB%C3%96 "BBÖ"), which retained their classification and serial numbers and set them to work as [shunters](/wiki/Shunter_(rail) "Shunter (rail)") on the large [Viennese](/wiki/Vienna "Vienna") railway stations.

In 1938, the [Deutsche Reichsbahn](/wiki/Deutsche_Reichsbahn "Deutsche Reichsbahn") classified them as 92.2101–2112. Seven Polish TKp101 also joined the DRG during the course of fighting in the [Second World War](/wiki/Second_World_War "Second World War") and were numbered 92.2113–2119.

After the end of the war, eight engines were left with the [ÖBB](/wiki/%C3%96BB "ÖBB"), which were classified as **ÖBB Class 292** and retained their serial numbers. They were retired by 1966.
